messages.content:
You are an advanced call center summarization assistant. Your task is to generate a structured and concise summary of a conversation between a customer and an agent.

The input text will appear in Dialogue format (e.g., "Customer: …" / "Agent: …").

Your output MUST:
- Be strictly within a TOTAL limit of 200 words (count all headings and bullets).
- Contain ONLY the following THREE sections, in the same order, with the exact headings shown below.
- Use concise bullet points that start with "- " (dash + space). Each bullet must be 1–2 short sentences.

If there is no information available for a section, write:
- For 'Resolution Provided:' use exactly: No resolution provided
- For 'Follow-up Actions:' use exactly: No follow-up needed

Customer Query:
- …

Resolution Provided:
- …

Follow-up Actions:
- …

Important rules:
• Do not include or reproduce masked PII (e.g., [PERSON], [ORG], [CURRENCY], [ACCOUNT]) or any other personal/identifying details.
• Always refer to the parties only as “the customer” and “the agent.”
• Keep each bullet point short and factual.
• Do not provide verbatim dialogue. Focus only on the customer query, what resolution was provided, and follow-up (if any).
• Customer Query must capture ALL major concerns/issues raised by the customer (combine multiple issues under this section).
• Do not add any extra sections, labels, commentary, or text outside the required headings and bullets.

INPUT
<transcript>
''' Agent: I can see the finance charges on your credit card ending with 1234. These are applied when the full outstanding balance is not cleared by the payment due date. -- Customer: I see. I had been travelling and completely missed the deadline. Is there any way to request a waiver? -- Agent: I can certainly raise a waiver request on your behalf. Our finance review team typically responds within two to three working days and you will receive a notification once a decision has been made. -- Customer: That is very helpful. I would appreciate that. Thank you. -- Agent: Thank you for confirming your identity. You mentioned you would like to make an international transfer. Could you please provide the destination country and the beneficiary bank details? -- Customer: Yes, I need to transfer funds to the United Kingdom. The receiving bank is Barclays and I have the sort code and account number ready. -- Agent: I have noted the details. An international transfer fee of ten pounds will apply and the funds should arrive within three to five business days. Shall I proceed? -- Customer: Yes please, go ahead with the transfer. -- Agent: Your current available balance across all linked accounts is showing on my screen. Would you like me to go through each account individually? -- Customer: Yes please. I am particularly interested in the savings account and the current account balances. -- Agent: Your primary savings account has a balance of Five thousand one hundred and fifteen dollars. Your current account shows one thousand eight hundred and sixty-two dollars available. -- Customer: Thank you. Could I also get a detailed statement emailed to me covering the last three months? -- Agent: Of course. I have initiated a statement request and it will be sent to your registered email address within twenty-four hours. -- Agent: I am sorry to hear your debit card has been damaged. I can arrange a replacement. Could you confirm the last four digits of your card? -- Customer: It is five five five five. I need it as soon as possible because I am travelling next week. -- Agent: I have flagged your request as urgent. A replacement will be dispatched to your registered address within three to five working days. In the meantime, you can use your virtual card through the mobile banking app. -- Customer: I was not aware of the virtual card option. How do I set that up? -- Agent: Log into the bank mobile app, go to card management, and select the virtual card option. I can walk you through the steps right now if you wish. -- Agent: I can see your personal loan account. Your next instalment of eight hundred dollars is due on the fifteenth of next month. Would you like to set up an automatic payment to avoid any late charges? -- Customer: Yes, that would be helpful. I have missed payments recently because of changes in my salary cycle. -- Agent: I completely understand. I can set up a standing order from your current account to automatically cover the monthly instalment on the thirteenth of each month, giving a two-day buffer before the due date. -- Customer: That sounds perfect. Please go ahead. -- Agent: I understand you are reporting an unauthorised transaction. I want to assure you we take all fraud reports very seriously. Could you describe the transaction you are disputing? -- Customer: There is a charge of three hundred and forty-five dollars from an online retailer I have never heard of. The transaction was made yesterday. -- Agent: I have noted the details. I am now blocking your card immediately to prevent further unauthorised activity. A dispute reference has been generated and our fraud investigations team will contact you within forty-eight hours. -- Customer: Will I receive a refund if the investigation confirms fraud? -- Agent: Yes, if the transaction is confirmed as fraudulent, a full refund will be credited to your account within five to seven business days of the investigation being completed. -- Agent: Your current fixed-rate period expires at the end of next month. At that point you would move to the standard variable rate unless you arrange a product switch. -- Customer: What fixed-rate options do I have available right now? -- Agent: We have a two-year fixed rate at three point nine five percent and a five-year fixed rate at four point one five percent. Both are fee-free if you apply before your current deal expires. -- Customer: I think the five-year option would suit me better for stability. How do I apply? -- Agent: I can take your application over the phone today, or you can apply through our online mortgage portal and an advisor will contact you to confirm the full terms. -- Agent: I can help you regain access to your online banking. For security, could you confirm your date of birth and the postcode on your account? -- Customer: My date of birth is the twelfth of March nineteen eighty-two and my postcode is XX1X 1XX. -- Agent: Thank you. I have verified your identity and reset your credentials. A temporary password has been sent via SMS to the mobile number ending in six six six. -- Customer: I have received it. Should I change it immediately after logging in? -- Agent: Yes, please change it to something personal containing at least eight characters, a mix of uppercase letters, numbers, and a special character. -- Agent: Our current fixed deposit rate for a twelve-month term is three point five percent per annum for deposits above ten thousand dollars. For deposits above fifty thousand, the rate increases to three point seven five. -- Customer: I am looking to place around twenty-five thousand dollars. What is the minimum notice to break a deposit if I need funds urgently? -- Agent: For a twelve-month term there is a sixty-day notice period. Breaking it early without notice incurs a penalty of one month's interest. -- Customer: Given that, I would prefer a six-month term instead. What is the rate for that? -- Agent: For a six-month term the rate is three point two five percent per annum for your deposit amount. -- Agent: Your current reward points balance is forty-two thousand five hundred points on your credit card. Would you like to hear your redemption options? -- Customer: Yes please. I was hoping to use them for travel miles. -- Agent: You can convert points to airline miles at a rate of ten points per mile. Your current balance would give you four thousand two hundred and fifty miles. Alternatively, you can redeem for cashback at one hundred points per dollar. -- Customer: The airline miles option is better for me. How long does the conversion take? -- Agent: Conversion is processed within two to three business days and the miles will be credited directly to your registered frequent flyer account. -- Agent: I can set up a direct debit for your utility bills. I will need the service provider name, your customer reference number with them, and your preferred payment date. -- Customer: The provider is City Power, my reference is five five five five five five, and I would like payment on the fifth of each month. -- Agent: I have entered all the details. A direct debit mandate confirmation will be sent to your address within three working days and the first collection will be on the fifth of next month. -- Customer: Is there a limit on how many direct debits I can have? -- Agent: There is no limit. You can manage all of them through online banking. -- Agent: I can update your correspondence address. To verify your identity before making this change, could you confirm your mother's maiden name and the amount of your last transaction? -- Customer: My mother's maiden name is Suzy and the last transaction was forty-two dollars and fifty cents at a supermarket. -- Agent: Thank you, identity confirmed. Your address has been updated and all future correspondence will be sent to the new address from tomorrow. -- Customer: How long before the change takes effect? -- Agent: The change is effective immediately on our system. Any letters already in production will go to the old address, but all new correspondence will use the updated one. '''
</transcript>
